title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Meta's new AI game maker
slides.explanation: Meta launched a new experimental app called Pocket. It lets you create and share your own mini games just by typing out a text prompt.

slides.headline: Anthropic eyes custom chips
slides.explanation: Anthropic is reportedly in talks with Samsung to build its own custom AI chips. This follows recent news that OpenAI is also working on its own chip technology.

slides.headline: Tesla driver faces charges
slides.explanation: A man in Texas has been charged with manslaughter after his Tesla crashed into a home and killed a woman. He told police he was using the car's driver assistance features at the time.

slides.headline: The Onion takes on Infowars
slides.explanation: The satirical site The Onion is moving forward with its takeover of Infowars. Their CEO says they plan to use the platform to mock conspiracy theories.

slides.headline: Zuckerberg on AI progress
slides.explanation: Mark Zuckerberg told his team that AI agent development is moving slower than he initially expected. He shared the update during a recent internal meeting.

caption: From new AI gaming tools to major updates on the chip industry, here is what is happening in tech today. We are breaking down the biggest headlines so you can stay in the loop. Swipe through to catch up on the stories that matter. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: Ryanair warns of airport travel delays
slides.explanation: Ryanair is asking European governments to delay a new border control system. They worry it will cause long queues for travelers during the busy summer season.

slides.headline: Singapore seizes mansion in chip probe
slides.explanation: Authorities in Singapore took control of a $42 million home. The move is part of an investigation into the illegal smuggling of servers containing AI chips.

slides.headline: Trump shares views on Federal Reserve
slides.explanation: Donald Trump criticized the Federal Reserve in a new interview. He also confirmed his plan to remove Lisa Cook from the central bank's board.

slides.headline: Meta says its AI is catching up
slides.explanation: A Meta executive claims their upcoming AI model is now on par with OpenAI. This marks a significant step in the ongoing competition between tech giants.

slides.headline: What to know about the Jersey Mike's IPO
slides.explanation: The sandwich chain is preparing to go public. Their recent filing gives a closer look at the company's financial growth and executive pay.

caption: From travel headaches in Europe to big shifts in the AI market, we have your daily finance update covered. Catch up on these top stories in just a few minutes. Check out the latest on Headlinne for more. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
